root_text.an5.80:1.1: “Pañcimāni, bhikkhave, anāgatabhayāni etarahi asamuppannāni āyatiṁ samuppajjissanti.
root_text.an5.80:2.2: Bhavissanti, bhikkhave, bhikkhū anāgatamaddhānaṁ cīvare kalyāṇakāmā.
root_text.an5.80:2.3: Te cīvare kalyāṇakāmā samānā riñcissanti paṁsukūlikattaṁ, riñcissanti araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni;
root_text.an5.80:2.4: gāmanigamarājadhānīsu osaritvā vāsaṁ kappessanti, cīvarahetu ca anekavihitaṁ anesanaṁ appatirūpaṁ āpajjissanti.
root_text.an5.80:2.5: Idaṁ, bhikkhave, paṭhamaṁ anāgatabhayaṁ etarahi asamuppannaṁ āyatiṁ samuppajjissati.
root_text.an5.80:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhavissanti bhikkhū anāgatamaddhānaṁ piṇḍapāte kalyāṇakāmā.
root_text.an5.80:3.2: Te piṇḍapāte kalyāṇakāmā samānā riñcissanti piṇḍapātikattaṁ, riñcissanti araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni;
root_text.an5.80:3.3: gāmanigamarājadhānīsu osaritvā vāsaṁ kappessanti jivhaggena rasaggāni pariyesamānā, piṇḍapātahetu ca anekavihitaṁ anesanaṁ appatirūpaṁ āpajjissanti.
root_text.an5.80:3.4: Idaṁ, bhikkhave, dutiyaṁ anāgatabhayaṁ etarahi asamuppannaṁ āyatiṁ samuppajjissati.
root_text.an5.80:4.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhavissanti bhikkhū anāgatamaddhānaṁ senāsane kalyāṇakāmā.
root_text.an5.80:4.2: Te senāsane kalyāṇakāmā samānā riñcissanti rukkhamūlikattaṁ, riñcissanti araññavanapatthāni pantāni senāsanāni;
root_text.an5.80:4.3: gāmanigamarājadhānīsu osaritvā vāsaṁ kappessanti, senāsanahetu ca anekavihitaṁ anesanaṁ appatirūpaṁ āpajjissanti.
root_text.an5.80:4.4: Idaṁ, bhikkhave, tatiyaṁ anāgatabhayaṁ etarahi asamuppannaṁ āyatiṁ samuppajjissati.
root_text.an5.80:5.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhavissanti bhikkhū anāgatamaddhānaṁ bhikkhunīsikkhamānāsamaṇuddesehi saṁsaṭṭhā viharissanti.
root_text.an5.80:5.2: Bhikkhunīsikkhamānāsamaṇuddesehi saṁsagge kho pana, bhikkhave, sati etaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ:
root_text.an5.80:5.3: ‘anabhiratā vā brahmacariyaṁ carissanti, aññataraṁ vā saṅkiliṭṭhaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjissanti, sikkhaṁ vā paccakkhāya hīnāyāvattissanti’.
root_text.an5.80:5.4: Idaṁ, bhikkhave, catutthaṁ anāgatabhayaṁ etarahi asamuppannaṁ āyatiṁ samuppajjissati.
root_text.an5.80:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhavissanti bhikkhū anāgatamaddhānaṁ ārāmikasamaṇuddesehi saṁsaṭṭhā viharissanti.
root_text.an5.80:6.2: Ārāmikasamaṇuddesehi saṁsagge kho pana, bhikkhave, sati etaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ:
root_text.an5.80:6.3: ‘anekavihitaṁ sannidhikāraparibhogaṁ anuyuttā viharissanti, oḷārikampi nimittaṁ karissanti, pathaviyāpi haritaggepi’.
root_text.an5.80:6.4: Idaṁ, bhikkhave, pañcamaṁ anāgatabhayaṁ etarahi asamuppannaṁ āyatiṁ samuppajjissati.
root_text.an5.80:7.1: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, pañca anāgatabhayāni etarahi asamuppannāni āyatiṁ samuppajjissanti.

translation_text.an5.80:1.1: “Mendicants, these five future perils have not currently arisen, but they will arise in the future.
translation_text.an5.80:1.2: You should look out for them
translation_text.an5.80:1.3: and try to give them up.
translation_text.an5.80:2.2: In a future time there will be mendicants who like nice robes.
translation_text.an5.80:2.3: They will neglect the practice of wearing rag robes and the practice of frequenting remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.
translation_text.an5.80:2.4: They will come down to the villages, towns, and capital cities and make their homes there. And they will employ many kinds of improper solicitation for the sake of a robe.
translation_text.an5.80:2.5: This is the first future peril that has not currently arisen, but will arise in the future …
translation_text.an5.80:3.1: Furthermore, in a future time there will be mendicants who like nice almsfood.
translation_text.an5.80:3.2: They will neglect the practice of walking for almsfood and the practice of frequenting remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.
translation_text.an5.80:3.3: They will come down to the villages, towns, and capital cities and make their homes there. And they will employ many kinds of improper solicitation for the sake of almsfood.
translation_text.an5.80:3.4: This is the second future peril that has not currently arisen, but will arise in the future …
translation_text.an5.80:4.1: Furthermore, in a future time there will be mendicants who like nice lodgings.
translation_text.an5.80:4.2: They will neglect the practice of staying at the root of a tree and the practice of frequenting remote lodgings in the wilderness and the forest.
translation_text.an5.80:4.3: They will come down to the villages, towns, and capital cities and make their homes there. And they will employ many kinds of improper solicitation for the sake of lodgings.
translation_text.an5.80:4.4: This is the third future peril that has not currently arisen, but will arise in the future …
translation_text.an5.80:5.1: Furthermore, in a future time there will be mendicants who mix closely with nuns, trainee nuns, and novice nuns.
translation_text.an5.80:5.2: In such conditions, it can be expected that
translation_text.an5.80:5.3: they will lead the spiritual life dissatisfied, or commit one of the corrupt offenses, or disavow the training and return to a lesser life.
translation_text.an5.80:5.4: This is the fourth future peril that has not currently arisen, but will arise in the future …
translation_text.an5.80:6.1: Furthermore, in a future time there will be mendicants who mix closely with monastery attendants and novices.
translation_text.an5.80:6.2: In such conditions it can be expected that
translation_text.an5.80:6.3: they will engage in storing up goods for their own use, and making obvious hints about digging the earth and cutting plants.
translation_text.an5.80:6.4: This is the fifth future peril that has not currently arisen, but will arise in the future …
translation_text.an5.80:7.1: These are the five future perils that have not currently arisen, but will arise in the future.
translation_text.an5.80:7.2: You should look out for them
translation_text.an5.80:7.3: and try to give them up.”
